diabetes: Watch for significant fluctuations in blood sugar levels. Avoid high-sugar foods.
hypertension: Monitor blood pressure regularly. Be cautious of high sodium intake.
heart disease: Consult your cardiologist before consuming this combination.
kidney disease: Ensure hydration. Limit potassium and phosphorus intake if applicable.
liver disease: Avoid alcohol and hepatotoxic substances entirely.
asthma: Be aware of potential respiratory distress or allergic reactions.
peptic ulcer: Avoid NSAIDs, alcohol, and spicy foods which may irritate the stomach lining.
thyroid disorder: Maintain consistent meal timing with levothyroxine and monitor TSH levels.
